decisionMemorySummary.note: Aggregates Codex recommendation snapshots compared against final human workbench decisions. Missing historical Codex recommendations remain unavailable and are not inferred.

meta.note: Codex-first conservative review. Human QC still required before merge.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image tags and top machine candidate suggest a 90 km/h sign but source options do not include 90; top candidates disagree (40/50/70/90). Optionset mismatch and no clear candidate match -> unresolved.

Scene shows roadworks/cones; candidate matches are mixed and top machine qids are unrelated (dashboard indicators). Cannot determine correct ROW answer from available candidates and visual/text signals.

Prohibition sign for horn clearly implies 'Yes' (use horn as little as possible), but top candidate q0334 shows conflicting correctRow data. Because source, image and machine candidates conflict, keep unresolved and flag risk.
Sign is a T-shaped intersection warning, but top candidate q0764 and q0693 disagree on option/key mapping; ambiguous candidate alignment and option-order differences — needs human check.

Scene shows a bus-only lane on right; statement 'possible to overtake using bus lane' is false. q0235 addresses using bus lane to overtake (correct = Wrong). Top machine match was unrelated (indicator light), so high risk flagged due to candidate conflict.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match (q0552) is about a dashboard light and unrelated to bus-lane overtaking; selected q0235 which explicitly covers bus-lane overtaking prohibition.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image tags and top machine candidate suggest a 90 km/h sign but source options do not include 90; top candidates disagree (40/50/70/90). Optionset mismatch and no clear candidate match -> unresolved.

Image shows a sharp curve; q0373's correct action is 'reduce speed and honk' which corresponds to the source French option A ('ralentir en klaxonnant'). Good semantic match despite candidate ranking variance.

Scene shows roadworks/cones; candidate matches are mixed and top machine qids are unrelated (dashboard indicators). Cannot determine correct ROW answer from available candidates and visual/text signals.

Prohibition sign for horn clearly implies 'Yes' (use horn as little as possible), but top candidate q0334 shows conflicting correctRow data. Because source, image and machine candidates conflict, keep unresolved and flag risk.

Blue rectangular tunnel sign with '2500 m' could indicate distance to entrance, distance to exit, or tunnel length depending on local sign convention; top candidates disagree. Ambiguous -> unresolved.
Sign is a T-shaped intersection warning, but top candidate q0764 and q0693 disagree on option/key mapping; ambiguous candidate alignment and option-order differences — needs human check.

Image shows a traffic light but candidate matches are inconsistent with the source option 'passage au rouge imminent' (amber meaning). No single qid clearly matches the pictured signal semantics — leave unresolved.
Image shows blue '60' sign (minimum speed). Candidate q0409 asks minimum speed with master correct = 60km/h; in this localized option order 60 km/h is source option B.
Prompt matches q0148 (overtaking parked vehicle on right). Master correct = keep safe lateral distance and reduce speed; localized option A ('ralentir et s'écarter du véhicule') corresponds to that.
Source principle ('wait and not overtake hastily at complicated intersection') aligns with q0092 (patiently wait); answer = Yes/Right. Candidate scores modest but semantically aligned.
Candidate q0368 concerns a 'small passenger vehicle' distance >100 m; source asks about a minibus. Family/numeric mismatch and insufficient evidence to safely reuse existing qid.
Visual shows roadside warning but top candidate is dashboard-indicator q0602 (mismatch). Prompt asks specific parking-distance — no clear matching qid found.
Diagram of intersection with yellow dashed lines likely about lane-connection markings but top candidates are mixed (dashboard indicators and cross-hatch q0804). Insufficient clear match to approve.

Image shows roadside parked car and question about why stopping is a violation (option D = on no-parking marking). No strong candidate qid match found; better to leave unresolved than create a possibly-incorrect mapping.

Prompt asks max speed on narrow road/bridge; q0374 specifically asks that and its correct answer is 30 km/h. In source options 30 km/h = D. Top machine candidate was q0142 (different topic), so selected q0374.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match (q0142) is about acceleration lanes/highway speed, not narrow road/bridge; q0374 is a better semantic match.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Dashboard lamp symbol ambiguous from image/text (could be master light switch, fog/rear fog, high/low beam). Not enough disambiguating visual detail to map to an existing qid.
Wording could refer to periodic formal technical inspection vs. a pre-drive safety check; cannot confidently map to existing qid or determine True/False without assuming legal context.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement matches q0072 which declares that maintaining the same speed with negligible distance does NOT prevent collision. Correct = Wrong/No (source B).

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Prompt and options match q0184 about driving in thick fog. q0184's correct behaviour ('hazard lights + fog light and stop') maps to the source French option C (options reordered), so approve q0184 with locale key C.

Prohibition sign for horn clearly implies 'Yes' (use horn as little as possible), but top candidate q0334 shows conflicting correctRow data. Because source, image and machine candidates conflict, keep unresolved and flag risk.
Source asks which card must be carried; extractedCorrectKey B = 'permis de conduire'. q0369 prompt/answers match ('driver's license'). Approve q0369 with locale key B.
Statement 'green light means allowed to proceed' directly matches q0645. ROW true => source option A (Yes).
Source statement equals q0048 ('A vehicle may stop on the ramp of a highway.') which is false; source answer should be No (B). Approve q0048 with locale key B.

Source statement matches q0332 ('Driving on the highway not allowed to exceed marked max speed'). ROW true => source option A (Yes).

Text match to q0341 ('Traffic police can detain vehicle if driver lacks ID') — legal statement is false; answer = No/Wrong. Match score modest; verify legally if needed.

Source principle ('wait and not overtake hastily at complicated intersection') aligns with q0092 (patiently wait); answer = Yes/Right. Candidate scores modest but semantically aligned.
Red circular sign with Chinese text indicating customs/frontier; master q0652 includes 'customs' and localized source option C = 'inspection de douane' matches that meaning.
Yellow triangular warning sign with bump/hump pictogram matches q0782 (speed bump ahead); localized correct = A.
Source statement ≃ candidate q0079 prompt (start/move at night → turn on low beam). French option A = Yes corresponds to master 'Right'.
Topic and correct meaning (reduce speed) match q0423. Master correct option maps to French option C (réduire sa vitesse) so return C.

Prompt about lawful driving practice for learners matches q0395. French option A corresponds to master correct choice (training vehicle with instructor).
Diagram of intersection with yellow dashed lines likely about lane-connection markings but top candidates are mixed (dashboard indicators and cross-hatch q0804). Insufficient clear match to approve.
Green rectangular sign with telephone and number (1212) visually and semantically matches q0761 (highway emergency phone). French option C = 'poste d'appel d'urgence' matches master.
Question about which lights when stopping in snow matches q0114; master correct = hazard lights. French option D = 'feux de détresse' => D.
Image of police giving arm signals and prompt asking meaning; q0880 candidate corresponds to 'turn left'. French option D = 'tourner à gauche' matches q0880 master.

Image shows roadside parked car and question about why stopping is a violation (option D = on no-parking marking). No strong candidate qid match found; better to leave unresolved than create a possibly-incorrect mapping.
Text-only question matches q0432 exactly (age limit options identical). Machine match is q0432 with correct answer '18~70 years'.
Image shows the prohibitory/give-way-to-oncoming-traffic sign (red circle, opposing arrows); q0854 matches and correct option = stop/yield (A).
Image shows a straight arrow and a left U-turn arrow; meaning 'straight or U‑turn'. q0848 candidate corresponds with U-turn answer (C).

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image of a stalk control lever; top candidate q0592 corresponds to windshield-wiper switch. In the French options 'essuie-glace' is D, so map master answer to source letter D. Some ambiguity with turn-signal stalks exists so confidence is moderate.
Statement about turning on indicator in advance and keeping distance when changing lanes matches ROW q0004 (true).
Image is a triangular warning sign indicating road narrows on both sides; q0680 matches with answer A.

Symbol is windshield wiper. Master q0603 correct = 'the windshield wiper' (master D) which corresponds to source option B ('l'essui-glace nettoyant le pare-brise avant').

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source claims a minor accident obstructing traffic 'does not need to move' — this is contradicted by safety rules. q0271 matches this scenario and marks such a claim as Wrong/False, mapping to source option B (No).
Candidate q0375 asks max highway speed when visibility <200m; master correct = 'less than 60 km/h' which corresponds to source option D (60 km/h). Match is plausible though machine scores are low.

Whether police may immobilize a vehicle for lack of technical-inspection certificate depends on precise legal provisions; machine matches are low-confidence and inconsistent. Require human/legal confirmation.
